blockingIssues.description: The feature claims to fulfill `VAL-ADD-002`, which requires the refined search bar styling to be consistent across Goods, Football, Basketball, and Concert pages, but the implementation only updates the goods search bar component and this commit touches no search components for the other tab pages.

Concert events only render a full-width banner when artistImageUrl is truthy. Seeded concert records use empty artistImageUrl and ConcertCard intentionally moved away from photo assets, so concert detail pages fall through to the generic sports gradient branch and show no concert-specific banner/image at the top.

The basketball redesign uses multiple arbitrary `drop-shadow-[...]` and `shadow-[...]` classes instead of the shared semantic shadow tokens required by the mission (`shadow-card`, `shadow-elevated`, etc.), so the page personality work is not fully aligned to the shared design system.

The profile menu icon tile background uses a dynamically constructed Tailwind class (`bg-${item.color.replace('text-', '')}-100`). Tailwind cannot statically detect this pattern, so the expected colored icon backgrounds will not be emitted in built CSS.
The admin login page still uses non-token gradient colors (`from-blue-50 to-cyan-50`) and a non-token `shadow-xl`, so the admin surface is only partially aligned with the shared design-token system.

The final integration pass is not fully evidenced: its transcript verifies `/my/order-record` while the actual route is `/my/orders`, and the cited commit is only the prior admin styling change rather than a durable integration artifact.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit rule forbidding dynamically constructed Tailwind utility classes and arbitrary shadow utilities when shared design tokens already exist.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: profile-pages-visual-polish used `bg-${item.color.replace('text-', '')}-100`, which Tailwind will not compile, and event-pages-visual-polish used several `shadow-[...]` / `drop-shadow-[...]` classes instead of shared shadow tokens.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how workers should authenticate agent-browser for middleware-protected routes and what fallback evidence is acceptable if visual verification is blocked; also require recording canonical route paths from the codebase during browser validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: product-event-detail-polish could not visually verify protected detail pages after redirects to `/login`, and cross-area-integration recorded `/my/order-record` instead of the actual `/my/orders` route while still reporting successful browser verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit final checks for semantic token compliance on polished pages and for verifying that every referenced test file actually exists before handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: login-register-visual-polish left `text-gray-*` / `bg-white` classes on the redesigned auth pages and its handoff implied broader auth-page test coverage than the repository contains.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document canonical profile sub-route names such as `/my/orders` in shared architecture guidance for browser-based validation work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cross-area-integration validated `/my/order-record` even though the real route is `app/my/orders/page.tsx`.

rejectedObservations.observation: Concert cards still do not render image-backed artwork and remain CSS-only.
